applicable_regime.explanation: Accrual date 2026-04-13 ≥ 2023-03-24 → HB 837 modified-comparative 51% bar applies (§768.81(6)).

fact_pattern_anchor.controlling_authority: Birge v. Charron, 107 So. 3d 350 (Fla. 2012); Pierce v. Progressive Am. Ins. Co.; Eppler v. Tarmac

doctrine_gates_applied.effect: Modified-comparative 51% bar for non-medical-negligence accruing after 2023-03-24.
Rear driver presumed at fault unless four named rebuttals evidenced.
doctrine_gates_applied.statute_or_case_cite: Birge v. Charron; Pierce; Eppler; Douglas-Seibert

rationale.net_apportionment_walk:
Start: rear_end anchor = 95% (rear_driver → insured_alvarez).
  insured_more_fault: 22.5 pts (police_report_narrative (hard_data) — DOC-008-CHP)
  insured_more_fault: 22.5 pts (police_report_field (hard_data) — DOC-008-CHP)
  claimant_more_fault: 7.5 pts (police_report_field (rebuttable_signal) — DOC-008-CHP)
  doctrine: hb_837_51_bar — §768.81(6); HB 837 (eff. 2023-03-24)
  doctrine: rear_end_rebuttable_presumption — Birge v. Charron; Pierce; Eppler; Douglas-Seibert
  doctrine: joint_several_abolished — §768.81(3)
Net per party: insured_alvarez=90.00%, insured_owner_cedar_ridge=2.50%, claimant_marini=7.50%

basis_evidence.quoted_span: Vehicle 1 (insured) failed to stop in time and struck Vehicle 2 (claimant), which was stopped in traffic.
Primary collision factor: Vehicle 1 (insured) — following too closely.
Vehicle 1 displayed magnetic signage reading 'QuickRoute Deliveries.'
Chief complaint: Neck and shoulder soreness after a low-speed motor-vehicle collision earlier today.

Mild left cervical paraspinal tenderness. Full range of motion. Neurologic exam intact. No midline tenderness. Cervical X-ray obtained — no acute fracture or malalignment. Assessment: Cervical strain (whiplash), minor. No red-flag findings.

change_conditions: If Birge-category rebuttal evidence lands (mechanical failure / sudden stop / lane-change-by-lead / illegal-stop), rear-driver anchor shifts down materially.
If poi_vs_claimant consistency check resolves (corroborated or contradicted), apportionment may shift.
If excess-judgment likelihood becomes clearer (damages evidence + low-limits confirmed), Powell duty to initiate tender solidifies.
Demand on file — sufficient-evidence assessment is the gating next decision; new evidence may flip the §624.155(4) call.
